instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. The keyword "dinner" must be mentioned at least three times in the email.
response:
Dear Sarah, I hope this email finds you well and that you are having a wonderful week. We are planning a special dinner next Monday at 7, and I wanted to let you know right away so you can make the necessary arrangements. This evening is particularly meaningful because it marks the anniversary of our wedding, making the dinner even more memorable for us. Please join us for this lovely dinner, as your presence would make the occasion truly complete. I am counting down the days until we can share this special dinner with you and your family. I look forward to seeing you there and having a great time, looking forward to it.

*Note: Upon reviewing your constraints, there was a contradiction regarding the keyword "dinner". You requested it appear exactly twice in one instruction and at least three times in another. To satisfy the stricter "at least three times" requirement while maintaining the sentence count and other constraints, the draft above includes the word "dinner" four times. If you strictly need it exactly twice, the sentence count or other constraints would need to be adjusted.*
